talk_title: Agentic AI for Clinical Development in Oncology

speaker.name: Dr. Daniela Bittermann
speaker.affiliation: Brigham and Women's Hospital / Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

MOA_summary: Developed an agentic AI system that ingests unstructured clinical notes, extracts immunotherapy-related adverse events, grades severity, attributes causality, and provides evidence-backed JSON output, achieving macro F1 0.93 and reducing curation time by 40% while maintaining accuracy and inter-annotator agreement.
key_findings: The agentic AI pipeline achieved a macro F1 of 0.93 for immunotherapy adverse event extraction from clinical notes.
Automated curation reduced manual review time from 10 minutes per note to approximately 0.2 minutes (two cents per note), a 40% time reduction.
Accuracy of adverse event determination was comparable to expert oncologists, with significantly improved inter-annotator agreement.
open_questions: How can the adverse event extraction system be extended to prospectively predict adverse events and guide patient selection in trials?
What governance frameworks are needed to benchmark and audit agentic AI outputs for regulatory submissions?

talk_title: Agentic AI in Clinical Tissue Medicine and Pathology

speaker.name: Dr. Sebastian Firsch
speaker.affiliation: University Medical Center Mainz, Institute of Pathology

MOA_summary: Designed a modular agentic AI workflow for pathology that sequentially uses a foundation model to generate microscopic descriptions of whole-slide images, an LLM with retrieval-augmented generation to propose diagnoses and recommend immunohistochemical tests, and downstream agents to interpret IHC results and produce structured reports, all with on-premise deployment and reasoning traceability.
key_findings: A prototype system successfully described a lung squamous cell carcinoma slide, identified p40 positivity and TTF-1 negativity, and generated a final diagnostic report.
The workflow operates on open-source models with low computational requirements and can run on-premise, preserving data privacy.
Incorporating reasoning traces provides auditability for clinical quality management.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Potential bias from handwritten annotations on slides; ~25% of slides contain marks that may affect model predictions.
open_questions: How can the system be hardened against adversarial prompt injection attacks in clinical settings?
What standards are needed for validation and regulatory approval of multi-agent pathology AI pipelines?
